understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting the selectable piece(s) onto all goal tiles marked with a '+', with every goal simultaneously occupied before the step counter reaches 0.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Only the currently-selected piece moves. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN moves the selected piece in that direction by a fixed stride (in my run it changed x by 3 each time: 18→15 and 9→12), presumably to the next valid stopping point. CLICK attempts to reposition the selected piece directly onto the clicked coordinate, but it only succeeds when the destination is valid/reachable under the rules (it worked for targets on the same row like [18,21], [15,21], [9,21], and failed when blocked like [21,21] or when clicking an unrelated location like [35,17]). If the destination is blocked by a wall/obstacle or otherwise invalid, the piece does not move. Each action consumes 1 step, and the run ends when steps reach 0.

orient_history: Movement seems to slide/step the currently selected piece by its size (3 pixels = 1 cell), but walls fully surround the selected piece so movement cannot start. Likely CLICK changes which selectable is active (or triggers a swap/interaction).

CLICK appears to directly reposition the selected 3x3 piece to the clicked (grid-aligned) pixel coordinate if that space is valid; since movement is blocked, winning likely requires CLICKing the piece onto the goal region.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts grid-step movement but current cell is enclosed by walls, so movement is impossible. CLICK attempts to move/teleport the currently selected piece to the clicked pixel location if that destination is valid (not blocked); clicking closer/more precisely on the goal may allow placement onto it.

CLICK likely attempts to teleport the selected 3x3 (1 cell) piece to the clicked pixel position, but only succeeds if the target is a valid grid-aligned (multiples of 3) empty/goal cell that doesn’t collide with walls. The earlier goal click may have failed due to misalignment (not on 3-pixel grid).

Clicks appear to change selection (or target interactions) but have not moved the piece onto the goal. It’s possible MOVE actions may still have effects even when ‘blocked’ (e.g., breaking/pushing walls or triggering a state change), which we haven’t tested yet.

Hypothesis: CLICK can only place/clone the selected piece onto a target position if the target is aligned in the same row or column (grid-aligned), possibly ignoring walls; MOVE is standard collision movement but current piece is trapped. To reach goals, we should CLICK onto goal tiles on the same row (y=7) first: pixels [33,21],[36,21],[39,21].

CLICK likely either (a) changes which selectable is active when clicking on a piece, or (b) attempts a teleport/move-to-clicked position but is constrained by collision/allowed cells. Since movement is fully blocked for the current selection, switching to the other selectable is the best way to make progress.

Hypothesis: CLICK either (a) selects a selectable if clicked on it, or (b) attempts to teleport the currently-selected 3x3 piece to the clicked top-left pixel coordinate if that placement is not colliding with walls/other pieces. Since the currently-selected piece at [9,21] cannot MOVE at all, progress likely requires switching selection to the other selectable and using it to reach the goal area (or to open space).

Hypothesis: CLICK performs a straight-line (rook-like) move when the target is in the same row/column and unobstructed by walls; if you CLICK on another selectable, it switches which piece is selected (as seen when clicking [9,21] and [21,21] previously). The piece at [21,21] is immobilized, so we must control the other piece to reach the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) if not blocked. CLICK does one of two things: (a) if clicking a selectable, it changes the selected piece to that selectable; (b) if clicking empty space, it attempts to move the selected piece there (possibly requiring a clear path / not crossing walls). To make progress, we should control the other selectable and see if it has a route toward the goal.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK is not a free-form move-to-target; it likely only swaps/teleports selection between predefined selectable positions. Normal MOVE_* is the real grid movement (3 pixels = 1 cell), but the currently selected piece is stuck in a walled pocket. I may need to change which piece is active (possibly via movement controls) to move the other piece toward the goal.

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is currently selected (earlier clicks on [9,21] and [21,21] seemed to change the selected piece). Since the current selected piece is fully blocked, I should switch to the other selectable and check if it has any available movement options toward the goal.

Movement with arrow actions moves the currently selected selectable by one cell (3 pixels) unless blocked by walls. CLICK on a selectable changes which piece is selected; to make progress we must control the movable piece and route it toward the goal region.

CLICK appears to change which selectable is active (selection), while MOVE_* translates the currently selected selectable by 3 pixels (1 cell) when not blocked by collisions. If the currently selected piece is truly stuck, switching selection may reveal a movable piece.
CLICKing on a selectable changes the currently controlled/selected piece (the 'player' reference), while MOVE_* moves only the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) if not blocked.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move the currently-selected piece directly to the clicked tile/object position if it is a valid empty/goal location; clicks on the other selectable are valid destinations, hence the back-and-forth teleport. Since MOVE is blocked, reaching the goal likely requires CLICKing onto the goal area to place a piece there.

New hypothesis based only on observed outcomes: MOVE_* attempts to shift the selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but is prevented by collidable walls/objects; CLICK does not move to arbitrary empty/goal tiles and instead likely only swaps/repositions relative to other selectables (since repeated CLICKs toggled between two selectable positions), so reaching the goal probably requires first escaping this boxed-in pocket via non-move mechanics on a later turn.

Hypothesis: CLICK only works when target_position is on the 3-pixel grid. If the clicked cell is empty/goal and not a wall, the selected piece teleports there; if the clicked cell contains another selectable, selection swaps (appearing as a move). Goal region appears at semantic cells around (x=12,y=6) => pixel (36,18).

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable piece at the clicked position (no movement). MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) if not blocked by walls. To reach the goal, we likely need to control the other selectable at [9,21] and navigate it (it may have different available moves).

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to teleport the currently selected 3x3 piece to the exact pixel coordinate (typically grid-aligned multiples of 3). If the destination overlaps a collidable wall/invalid space, the piece doesn’t move. Since the goal’s anchor is not grid-aligned, I should try other grid-aligned points within the goal’s 5x5 area (x in {36,39}, y in {18,21}).

Hypothesis: CLICK can move/teleport the selected piece, but only if the click lands on a valid destination pixel region (e.g., the interior/center of the goal area). Previous goal clicks may have missed the valid hotspot even though they were near/inside the goal rectangle.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* only works if adjacent cell is empty; both selectable positions may be separate pockets. CLICK on the other selectable position swaps the selected piece into that other pocket (possibly swapping the two pieces). To make progress, we need to swap into the other pocket and see if it has any open movement toward the goal.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): this level is a placement/selection puzzle where normal movement is mostly impossible because pieces start in wall-locked sockets; progress likely requires a non-CLICK action to trigger a different mode/state (e.g., swap/shift between sockets or cycle selection) rather than attempting to pathfind to the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches the active/selected piece (engine reports this as the 'selected piece moved'). Arrow moves apply only to the currently selected piece; one of the two pieces may have available movement or interactions needed to reach the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICKing on a selectable switches which piece is selected; CLICKing elsewhere attempts to reposition the currently selected piece but may fail if blocked/invalid. Since goal-area CLICKs failed with the current selection, we should try selecting the other piece and then attempt goal placement.

CLICK appears to move the selected piece in a straight line (same row/column) until it reaches the target or is stopped by a wall; clicking on the other selectable swaps positions. Plan: slide right to the edge of the blocking wall, then go up above the wall’s top, then right into the goal columns, then down onto the goal row.

Based on observed history, CLICK seems to swap/teleport only when clicking certain interactable tiles; previous clicks on other selectable positions caused the selected piece to change position, while clicks on non-interactable/wall areas did nothing. Hypothesis: clicking the exact goal object's anchor pixel may be required to teleport/place the selected piece onto the goal region.

CLICK appears to select a different selectable (the log reports this as the 'selected piece moved' because selection changes to the piece at the clicked position). Arrow moves move the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) unless blocked by walls.

New hypothesis: CLICK may not be the primary way to solve this; it might only relocate/select within limited positions, while MOVE_* could be the real global mechanic (e.g., shifting pieces together or advancing some hidden state) even if the currently-selected piece appears blocked. I need to test MOVE actions to see if anything else changes besides the selected piece.

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is currently controlled (the 'player' reference), while MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected selectable by 3 pixels (1 cell) if not blocked. To make progress, we likely must control the other selectable and try moving it toward any open space.

Clicks appear to command the currently selected piece to move directly to the clicked coordinate if the engine allows it (possibly ignoring normal stepwise movement in some cases); with no legal MOVE_* available, the only remaining chance is a click that might place the piece onto the goal area.

Actions consume steps; when steps reach 0 the engine will not allow any further movement/teleport, so we cannot reach the goal from the current trapped position. CLICK seems to attempt a direct move/teleport to a clicked coordinate but is blocked by obstacles/constraints, and with 0 steps it likely cannot change state.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting the selectable piece(s) onto all goal tiles marked with a '+', with every goal simultaneously occupied before the step counter reaches 0.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Only the currently-selected piece moves. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN moves the selected piece in that direction by a fixed stride (in my run it changed x by 3 each time: 18→15 and 9→12), presumably to the next valid stopping point. CLICK attempts to reposition the selected piece directly onto the clicked coordinate, but it only succeeds when the destination is valid/reachable under the rules (it worked for targets on the same row like [18,21], [15,21], [9,21], and failed when blocked like [21,21] or when clicking an unrelated location like [35,17]). If the destination is blocked by a wall/obstacle or otherwise invalid, the piece does not move. Each action consumes 1 step, and the run ends when steps reach 0.
